question: When Jake was walking with Nicous and Tasha carrying a package behind the houses, and Alice was listening to sad love songs and finishing a star, what was Shure doing?
answer: Cleaning up the trash and packing it into a box while chatting with Katrina.
caption_text: I started out in the yard, standing up and adjusting my glasses to get a better look. After taking deliberate steps forward, I picked up a box and carefully placed my guitar into its bag. I took a moment to tidy the yard a bit, organizing things as I went before settling down and tuning in to the song "Shure: (Singing) Saying goodbye, I want to leave but don't dare to look back" on my phone. As the music filled the air, I sang along to "Some fall, some go crazy, some go with the wind, I feel sad," and announced I was singing New Pants' songs, remarking that you need a lisp to truly capture their essence. Katrina was nearby, occupied at the table. I spent a while on my phone, scrolling through what looked like short videos, listening and singing along, reciting the lyrics "Forget that rock music, the era of unyielding spirit." Then, I put my phone down and moved onto a tablet, listening to a song I enjoyed and occasionally glancing at the lyrics. Katrina was still there, occupied with her own stuff, and a whiteboard with writings and drawings was to my left. I fiddled with some papers on the table while listening to "New Pants" on the tablet, then picked up my phone to respond to some messages. "Shure" was asking about Jake's payment, and I scrolled through my contacts, seemingly looking for funds, typing a message about red envelope limits. The conversation involved Katrina transferring 300 yuan because the first day was only 150. I then scrolled through my friend circle on my phone, whistling in amusement and examining photos, before looking up when Katrina asked who came over today, confirming it was "the third boy" from Jinan. I looked over as Katrina said, "Yes, yes" before I said "Shure: Isn't he the second boy?". Katrina clarified that she's only been in one relationship, but could do one more briefly. I jokingly told her to hurry, then announced we should clean up and grabbed my phone. "Shure" sent some funny messages about his girlfriend's makeup mishap, which had me laughing, sending back a "Yes, mom" and walking around, cleaning up trash, and sending voice messages via WeChat. Putting my phone down, I jokingly said "I can't take it anymore," to Katrina as I piled trash into my box, imitating her and claiming it as my own before walking towards the door to put a box down. I then walked back towards a table, tossing a balled-up piece of trash into it as I sang, "I will marry him. Build something at every corner he walks. Girls who have been together for a long time are also beautiful. Forget about rock music". As the lyrics from "The era of galloping is gone" started up I commented on how long it's been since I've sun-dried clothes. Next I was talking about dryer use before focusing on a whiteboard, reminiscing with Katrina about living in Shahe and moving dorms multiple times, recounting that S6 dorm was the best when we first arrived at the new school. Then I heard Katrina talking about "a small concrete ground" and then Shure smelled BBQ. Next I was sitting down while looking at a tablet and exclaimed "What the heck?". I then laughed and started singing the lyrics "What makes you sad" as Katrina did something with her hands. I was watching a video that Katrina says is her favorite from elementary school when I say the lyrics from the video, "Forget it, just go away." The video on the tablet also had us saying "Life is like the sea" and I then went on the tablet, trying to find certain content about "Life is like the sea" and "It was a birthday gift" while Katrina chatted about reading "How the Steel Was Tempered" back then.

question: When Jake was walking with Nicous and Tasha carrying a package behind the houses, and Alice was listening to sad love songs and finishing a star, what was Shure doing?
answer: Cleaning up the trash and packing it into a box while chatting with Katrina.
caption_text: So, we were out making deliveries, and man, I had a ton of packages! I was walking with Jake, Nicous, and Tasha, and we were all pretty burdened. Someone had apparently stolen a lighter from Jake's roommate, which was kind of funny, and we were constantly trying to figure out how to manage all the stuff we were carrying. Jake kept laughing about some situation with Shure talking big, and we passed some parked tricycles. Later, as we walked into an alley, I heard Jake musing about people watching our packages. There were fewer glasses than expected. Eventually, we made it to the main road and I was starting to feel the weight of everything. Tasha joked that the packages were up to my neck, and Jake was saying something about how bad it would have been if we'd had to pick them up yesterday. As we walked, I mentioned how long it takes me to pick up and open packages and how exhausted I was. I even asked when we were getting paid! We headed back the way we came, discussing payment and whether Jake was speeding. We passed by a red car and talked about how people can run 5 km an hour. Tasha was exhausted, and it was hot out there; then, we detoured to check out a lottery shop after Jake joked about not getting sunburned and making money. The lottery shop ended up being closed, so we joked about saving money and continued. Tasha wasn't happy about walking with my feet. Then we had a hilarious conversation about eating KFC on behalf of other people, which led to a slight package mishap when Nicous' package fell and I threw mine down, resulting in a complete "reallocation of resources." To improve our carrying capacity, I then proceeded to tuck a flower into my waist and then put my hand in Jake's pocket and grabbed a power bank. Finally, our conversation turned to whether a certain person was going to attend an event or eat much, which concluded the delivery.

question: Which of the following happened last? A) Jake systematically scanned all rooms with his phone to document the space B) Tasha multitasked on her phone and instructed refrigerating dessert before the meal, C) Katrina settled on a simple upbeat dance during pancake cooking, D) Alice filmed Shure’s drum game and tried the claw machine, E) Lucia coordinated cake layering and joined the group for outdoor dining,
answer: A
caption_text: I noticed a power bank lying on the floor and overheard Tasha commenting on how "ugly" the mixture was, mistakenly thinking it was made with cake flour when it was actually glutinous rice flour. Alice wondered what glutinous rice flour was and Katrina asked if they should bring Shyness when dancing, Tasha then chimed in with "At home, we usually use glutinous rice flour." I looked back at the power bank. Lucia was talking about needing things for dance practice and thought at least when they practice, everyone should bring it, and there was a continuing discussion about how a male dancer joining their group would impact their dance style. Later, I saw Lucia talking about relationships, while Jake, Tasha, Katrina, and I weighed the pros and cons of someone joining and Lucia was championing the idea of more dancers, while walking around her stool behind her. I heard Katrina mention looking for something and wanting to ask her classmates about what they danced to before, and Tasha commented that it felt like a big fail, hahaha. I walked to the front of the table, spotted some trash on my desk, picked it up, and heard them discussing adding seasoning, sugar, and choosing clothes to wear. I discarded the trash in a can on the side of the living room, and then picked up my phone and two boxes, placing the boxes on the bed in my bedroom. Back in the living room, they were making something sweet, and were discussing sugar syrup, saving budget, and the end result being too bitter. I went to the living room and heard them counting coffee orders. Tasha wanted milk, and I spoke with her about it and laughed. Katrina asked for the Wifi password. Jake was having trouble connecting, then he and Lucia mentioned that there were two people blocking. I spoke with Tasha some more. Next, Jake was pointing to a whiteboard, where Alice said she'd have coffee, but there was only one slot left. I observed everyone and then headed back to my bedroom, but turned back around when I heard Alice say that she wanted to have one. I then observed Lucia, Alice, Jake, Tasha, and Katrina in the living room. There was laughter about a message being withdrawn and resent. Lucia asked about milk drinkers and finding a small pot. After exiting the door, I turned back around, went to the side of the table, picked up my phone, and checked my WeChat messages. Jake messaged about contacting garbage collection. There was also a message about finding a B&B, iodine, alcohol and Katrina mentioned a simple dance for annual parties. I started chatting with them, saying "Jake: Yes." Sitting at the table, I asked Lucia and Katrina if a certain song was popular on TikTok, they said it was a classic. Alice offered to stir something, and Jake asked about the song's origin. I continued to play on my phone, while the others asked "How long?" and Lucia and Katrina discussed simple movements, wearing glasses, and doing styled poses. Tasha wanted to take a look at something, Katrina proposed a tree headpiece, and Tasha asked if that was dorayaki. I was still replying to messages on my phone. Lucia said someone had loaded something out. Katrina was watching videos on her tablet, asking if people danced, and Katrina mentioning it was rock music that Master's favorite. I then heard Tasha talking about glutinous rice flour and not wanting to study anymore and saw Katrina picking up a cup. I was still looking at my phone, Lucia was dancing and mentioned that something looked funny, which made Jake laugh. I asked if they wanted to clean the room, but Tasha just wanted to sift through it. They were talking about switching formations. Lucia asked if they should tidy up and scan, Alice said the consistency wasn't good for sifting. I spoke with Lucia and listened to Jake and Katrina talk about how switching looked better. I continued to look at my phone. There was talk of sweeping, particles, and tidying. Tasha said something looked like cheese and that the net they bought was too dense and shouted, “Damn it, Mashaka”. I stood up and walked toward Katrina and Alice. While Katrina and Lucia had a conversation about SOLO, removing the "boys' positions" and that "we don't have that many people anyway", Alice said that "this looks about right". I picked up my phone and looked at the screen, replying to a message from a colleague, and heard them talking about girl group dances, idol dances, sifting takoyaki, wanting more hype songs, someone not having iodine, ordering something on Meituan, and a "Vietnam ride". I heard them talking about starting to cook, something about "Building 53," Tasha opened the cabinet to find oil, and Alice pointed out where it was. Back in the kitchen, Tasha asked for a spatula, and Alice asked if it could handle high heat. Finally, I turned back to Katrina, who was dancing and saying it felt like a workout. I complained about a spatula being too oily, noticed Alice walking into the living room, and then heard her saying to just drink something cold, no need to heat it up. As the camera's perspective shifted, I turned to observe Alice engaged in conversation with Lucia. I then walked over to join them, where I heard Lucia playfully remark about the milk getting hot anyway, but Alice rejected it. Tasha then interjected, declining the hot milk and not wanting it put near her. Lucia asked if Tasha wanted to use a certain pot, but Tasha clarified she just wanted to put something there for a moment and Lucia told her she would find another pot to pour something into. I stepped off a stool, observed Lucia rummaging through a cabinet, and heard Lucia commenting about a pot being too big for heating milk. Alice said she would wash a cup and that she didn't drink hot milk, as Tasha went to retrieve some paper towels before heading back to the kitchen. Lucia then said "Let me check again." , followed by her and Alice talking about if they were all using the same cups. I then headed to a table with a red and white checkered pattern, picked up a phone and put it down. I picked up my phone from the table, overheard them in the kitchen area discussing the use of a large pot for cooking pancakes, concerns about its size for heating milk, and whether too much oil was added. They mentioned surprising foreigners, using a pot for the first time for pancakes, and leaving the excessive oil as is. I started walking away from the kitchen, headed towards the staircase, ascended to the second floor, and stopped at the doorway, looking at my phone. After having a brief conversation with colleagues, I turned off my phone, proceeded to the living room, walked to the sofa, sat down, and picked up a power bank from the floor to check its battery level.

caption_text: it's Day 2 around 10:50 AM and I'm hanging out in the kitchen with everyone. Tasha's making something with low-gluten flour – it seems like a pancake or maybe even dorayaki – and I'm helping stir, though I don't really know what I'm doing, just following instructions. Alice is leading the charge on the cooking, though even she admits she's not great at sweet stuff. The conversation's all over the place, from whether Shure should join our dance practice (Katrina and Lucia were debating pros and cons), to deciding on clothing choices, to whether something tastes bitter, even after adding milk. Then we switched gears, and I offered to count who wanted coffee. Alice changed her mind a few times, and the wifi password became an issue, with Jake complaining it was blocked. We then went back to the cooking, discussing whether sugar would dissolve properly, and I continued stirring. Later, the focus shifted to the dance practice, with Lucia, Katrina, and Jake looking at a tablet and laughing at different ideas, and for a moment, the conversation was dorayaki. The group was talking about everything from dance lessons to rock music. From there we had to change the container for the mix. We were debating whether to clean the room first and get organized, or just focus on the food. Tasha thought the mesh we were using to sift the flour was too fine. Then, while the girls were lying down with my friends, they started chatting girl group dances or idols. Tasha finally started cooking, and I watched her tap her phone to exit the video. Then, a discussion about hot vs. cold milk, including the need to heat up a cup. We started using smaller cups, then I washed a cup at the sink. Finally, Tasha started cooking something in a pan, so I washed and cleaned a cup. I told her to turn on the range hood because of the smoke, and checked to make sure the flame wasn't too high while Tasha was cooking the egg mixture, and I was making sure she was being careful.

question: Which of the following happened last? A) Jake systematically scanned all rooms with his phone to document the space B) Tasha multitasked on her phone and instructed refrigerating dessert before the meal, C) Katrina settled on a simple upbeat dance during pancake cooking, D) Alice filmed Shure’s drum game and tried the claw machine, E) Lucia coordinated cake layering and joined the group for outdoor dining,

caption_text: The video starts with me scrolling on my phone, waiting for a download to finish while everyone's discussing some kind of cooking experiment. Tasha's wondering about the flour, and I remember that glutinous rice flour might be what we need. Then, the conversation shifts to dancing, with Katrina suggesting we include Shure and wondering how adding a guy would change the dance style; I think he should join to add power. Then Katrina says something about embarassing ourselves together, and I tidy my clothes and turn on my phone while the others chat about uniform style and sugar in coffee. I offered coffee to everyone, prompting a conversation about saving money and whether it's even worth it. After checking to see how many people wanted coffee, which required everyone to raise their hands, then hearing Alice want a refund for her previously ordered coffee, I get us all sorted and decide I'm not particularly sleepy. So then the conversation segues into the need to contact a garbage collector and the need for first aid supplies. We then start to think about dance moves, and Katrina finds one that she says is very simple and shows it to me on her tablet, and tells Jake and I that it is the dance that people do at annual parties. As we examine Katrina's tablet, discussing the song and the dance, the suggestion is made to "strike some poses," I fix my bangs as Katrina jokingly demonstrates and says poses of striking a pose like a tree. After the dance poses conversation, Tasha then asks if dorayaki is made with low-gluten flour, and I pull up the Meituan app on my phone to look it up. Katrina asks me if I dance and shows me some dance videos for ideas. I see one I like and jump up, trying to copy the moves, before we all discuss formations and consider changing rooms to practice, which Katrina likes best because it "has a bit of hip-hop elements." Eventually, Jake starts thinking about sweeping, so they can help us sweep first, then tidy up. Tasha comments that the net is too dense, while I observe with happiness and rhythm while tapping. After Katrina asks "Why is it solo again", I express my opinion that the dance is very good and recommend just taking out the boy's positions. Then we begin looking for another dance. We start talking about sifting being really hard and remember what a struggle it was to make Takoyaki with it. Then I order iodine on Meituan, if it turns out they dont have some. Then Katrina comments that our future career. With dancing in mind, Katrina starts walking toward the bathroom area, while I am looking at my phone. While there, Katrina remarks that the dancing "feels like a radio exercise", while Tasha is trying to figure out the oily spatula problem, with Alice saying "I'll just drink it cold, no need to heat it up for me". Which sparks me to start preparing to heat milk in the kitchen, but Alice says she wants to drink the milk cold and doesn't want to heat it up. While grabbing the milk, I say "Ah, don't put it here" with Tasha saying something to me. I am then about to get more milk, when I look for a small pot but can't find one. But eventually find one in a cabinet. As Alice washes dishes, I get ready to clean a pot to heat the milk in and watch as she comes towards it. Finally, after Tasha says: "Ah, okay, okay, it's smoky", I carefully clean the pot, enjoying a moment of common leisure, before rinsing it off and watching as Alice comes towards it.

caption_text: we started out watching a dance video together on a tablet. The music was a bit kindergarten-ish, but apparently good for a dance that everyone knows, although a bit too slow for a selfie. I was moving along with the rhythm and noticed Katrina was leaning in closer, which felt nice. We were laughing and enjoying the shared experience, but then we started thinking about pouring the milk, so I went to the kitchen to get cups and pour water and milk. Alice and Tasha were also around and talking about flipping something over on the heat. After, I noticed that we were searching for solo dances on the tablet, but had trouble finding them. We talked about how the music in a video sounded and Katrina decided to search for one called "Running Youth". After a brief search of "Youth Running", we were watching a dance video with others, discussing the dance moves and figuring out if they were too difficult for everyone to learn. Katrina and Lucia thought some moves might need to be removed or rearranged, and Katrina nodded in agreement to the change. So we replayed the first minute and a half of a video, because it was found that most of what we needed in the video was only the front of the clip, where the dancers began breaking down the moves. Lucia and Katrina were really focused on the video, so I left to get a power bank. When I came back we thought about where the dancing should take place. We argued about whether to dance outside or on the second floor. Both Katrina and I were hesitant about both the gravel and limited space available. So, we decided to head outside to examine the venue. Outside on the wooden board, we chatted with Jake about where to position ourselves and how to arrange the dance formation since the space was limited; figuring out if we could stand in two rows, or if anyone would fall if we tried to use the area below the wooden deck. Then Lucia asked about moving a projector outside. Jake pointed out that he can pick up some fabric for about 20 bucks and we joked about placing it on the stone site. Finally, inside, we were trying to decide on what colors or patterns would go on it when we sat back down at the table with the tablet, glancing around at the others. I was sitting at the red and white checkered table, picking up glasses of milk and taking sips between the "Youth Running" dance videos. I realized we also needed the chopsticks. Then Jake started complaining about me dissing him and not replying to his messages, and I overheard him talking about how he wanted me to eat breakfast. The others were setting out bowls and chopsticks, with some milk, coffee and sugar. There was also food I was smelling. I then got up and said I'd grab another bowl and Alice informed me that she'd already washed all of the bowls. Alice then told me to wipe the chopsticks before using them.

caption_text: I am looking at my phone, and I open the WeChat application. I'm reading messages in the chat. The messages say, "Jake: Hey, let's contact the garbage collection first," followed by, "Lucia: Maybe we can ask them about the B&B here."
